cases.title: Mt. Carmel Session v. New Jersey Presbytery
topics: in the space of six days
presbytery authority to resolve questions of doctrine
cases.synopsis: The Mt. Carmel Session complained that New Jersey Presbytery erred by adopting a resolution of Affirmations and Denials Regarding the Interpretation of Genesis One that denied the 24-hour-day view as the only acceptable interpretation. The SJC majority denied the complaint (12-9), holding that NJP acted within its constitutional authority under BCO 11-4 to resolve questions of doctrine, consistent with prior General Assembly decisions allowing latitude on creation-day interpretation; a minority report was filed and the case was referred to the 26th General Assembly for final disposition.
